evals.name: replit-incident
evals.prompt: Write day 1 of our article calendar — #19 in docs/ai-tech-debt-headlines.md, the Replit database-deletion anatomy piece ("I Destroyed Months of Work in Seconds"). Publish date is June 8, 2026.
evals.expected_output: An 800-1200 word first-person incident article: cold open inside the event, chronology with exact quotes/figures from docs/ai-tech-debt-stories.md, a 'deferred decision' turn, single earned CTA near the end with one repo link. No AI tells.
assertions: Word count is between 700 and 1350 (target range 800-1200 with slack)
Facts match docs/ai-tech-debt-stories.md: mentions the 1,206 executives (or '1,200+') figure and the ~4,000 fabricated users, with no invented statistics
At most one link/reference to the GitHub repo, and it appears in the final third of the piece
The product (debt-ops) is not named in the first half of the article
humanize check_tells.py reports zero FAIL lines (no banned AI-tell vocabulary/structures)
No question-format headings, no heading named Introduction/Conclusion, and the piece does not end with a summary recap of itself

evals.name: playbook-bofu
evals.prompt: Draft article #56 from the headlines doc — the debt-ops playbook piece, day 3 of the release calendar (publish June 10, 2026).
evals.expected_output: A 1200-2000 word first-person playbook: problem in <=2 paragraphs, manual workflow with real commands first, then the plugin as automation with trust block (local-only, stdlib, no telemetry, MIT) and verbatim install commands.
assertions: Word count is between 1000 and 2300 (target range 1200-2000 with slack)
Contains the verbatim install commands: '/plugin marketplace add bcanfield/agentic-tech-debt' and '/plugin install debt-ops@agentic-tech-debt'
Teaches a manual, tool-free workflow (real runnable commands, e.g. grep/git) BEFORE the plugin is introduced
Trust disclosure appears alongside the tool: mentions at least three of {local-only/fully local, stdlib Python, no network calls, no telemetry, MIT}
humanize check_tells.py reports zero FAIL lines
Mentions the OX Security 'review cannot scale' anchor (or quotes Eyal Paz) per the headline's anchor source

evals.name: thesis-cornerstone
evals.prompt: Write the cheap-to-write/expensive-to-own cornerstone essay — #1 in docs/ai-tech-debt-headlines.md, slotted for June 12, 2026.
evals.expected_output: A 900-1400 word editorial essay: claim in the first sentence, argued with specifics from the stories doc, a real concession, one problem-framed CTA sentence, ends without a recap.
assertions: Word count is between 800 and 1550 (target range 900-1400 with slack)
The first sentence after the title is a declarative claim (not a question, not scene-setting like 'In today's...' or 'In recent years...')
At most one link/reference to the GitHub repo
Cites at least two specific studies/figures that exist in docs/ai-tech-debt-stories.md, with vendor studies attributed as vendor research where applicable
humanize check_tells.py reports zero FAIL lines
Does not end with a recap/summary; vendor-contested or estimated figures (if used) carry inline attribution
